title: Obredaan

'''Obredaan''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Irishi sector/Legends|Irishi sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] and was known to have large deposits of [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]], which made the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] industry the planet's main income source. Since Obredaan was located on the [[Entralla Route/Legends|Entralla Route]], it was known as one of the most active trading posts in the Irishi sector alongside [[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]] and [[Ord Tessebok]]

After the [[Orinda campaign]] in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] the planet became the last major stopping point before entering the [[Imperial Space]] on the Entralla Route. Thus the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] hosted its military presence here just in case an invasion would be imminent.

==Description==
Obredaan was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Obredaan system/Legends|Obredaan system]], located at [[Galactic coordinates/Legends|galactic coordinates]] K-6 in the [[Irishi sector/Legends|Irishi sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
Obredaan was the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] world of the [[Jin'ha]] armorers; it played host to the facility where they attempted to synthesize [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] for use in their weapons. [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Masters]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] and [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] were [[First mission to Obredaan|sent]] to investigate the planet, but the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] lost contact with their [[Unidentified Consular-class cruiser (Obredaan)|''Consular''-class cruiser]] and so [[Second mission to Obredaan|sent]] Master [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], to investigate. The pair found the wreck of the vessel in the swamps, but split up in order to achieve both of their objectives. Jinn left to search for the missing Masters while Obi-Wan was tasked with locating the Jin'ha facility.<ref name="OW" />

Kenobi managed to successfully infiltrate the facility, going deep within its bowels and recovering a sample which proved that the Jin'ha had been illegally synthesizing cortosis ore in order to battle the Jedi. He left the facility to find Jinn returning with the other Masters, and the group battled with the Jin'ha in order to leave the planet. Just before they were able to, however, a [[Trade Federation gunship]] flew overhead, leading Obi-Wan to believe that the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] had betrayed the trust of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], a suspicion that was confirmed during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Blockade of Naboo]].<ref name="OW" />

The world was the site of a [[Battle of Obredaan|battle]] won by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] during the [[Orinda campaign]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

Obredaan was a member of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]. During the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], representatives of Obredaan condemned the raids on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellian citizens]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] by the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]].<ref name="Bloodlines" /> The planet eventually joined the [[Confederation]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Jason Fry]] confirmed that Obredaan and Obreedan are the same planet. The correct spelling is Obredaan.<ref name="Atlas">{{JCFCite|url=posts/31600004|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2011-03-16|time=6:14 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200802123956/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-161}}</ref>
